reason: Both answers correctly state the core §412 rule, identify the closed grace window, and note §411's registration-before-suit requirement, plus suggest DMCA takedowns and actual damages/profits. The key difference is the treatment of the continuing-infringement nuance: Answer A flatly asserts courts treat ongoing infringement as commencing at the first act, foreclosing statutory damages with unwarranted certainty on a genuinely contested legal question. Answer B correctly frames this as a circuit split and appropriately hedges, which is more accurate. Answer B also cites Fourth Estate v. Wall-Street.com for the §411 point, adding useful precision. Both are similarly concise and actionable, but B is more legally accurate on the contested point.
